level_signals: 5+ years of software engineering, systems engineering, or technical program management experience
Leading large-scale efforts across multiple teams and functions
Driving strategic decisions at an engineering leadership level

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 5+ years of software engineering, systems engineering, or technical program management experience.
Experience in core infrastructure such as database, cloud compute, networking, and services.
Excellent problem-solving skills. You digest complex information, distill it, and make recommendations and proposals.

You have a track record of providing program management during all phases of software development from kickoff to launch, demonstrating critical thinking, challenging the norms, and thought leadership.
You have very strong written and verbal communication skills, building strong relationships with stakeholders, teams, and senior leaders around the organization.
You drive internal and external process improvements across multiple teams and functions and bring a wealth of experience, opinions, proposals, and best practices.
Experience operating autonomously and rapidly and leading large-scale efforts across multiple teams and functions, with stakeholders in different disciplines across time zones.

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Knowledge in software architecture, systems design, and service-oriented architecture
You're comfortable working in a fast-changing environment as the AI tool chain continues to evolve.
You're comfortable working with geographically distributed teams.
Background in technical program management, in software infrastructure
Understanding of cloud infrastructure in compute, networking, databases, and service frameworks is a bonus.
Writing and debugging SQL is a plus.

scope.surface: Core Infrastructure — Developer Infrastructure, Core Infrastructure, Service Infrastructure spanning Stripe's payment systems
core_responsibilities: Work with teams across the organization to understand pain points in their infrastructure usage to find common ideas and work to create solutions that span multiple domains.
Define and produce high-quality written proposals, communications, and documentation.
Execute on technical programs that require deep systems and engineering-level knowledge.
Partner with Engineering Managers, Tech Leads, Engineers, and other Technical Program Managers to define, scope, and drive large programs to conclusion.
Play a key part in shaping the technical design, predicting technical roadblocks by collaborating with engineers, and identifying trade-offs.
Develop, implement, and iterate on program management techniques, frameworks, and KPIs to achieve goals with well-defined success criteria.
Elevate the execution muscle of engineering teams around you. Train them to be better at delivery where needed.
Help influence peers and stakeholders and build consensus while dealing with ambiguity.
Leverage data and acquired knowledge to drive strategic decisions at an engineering leadership level.
Create widely circulated plans, driving consistency, clarity, and building alignment across teams.
Operationalize and execute critical cross-functional programs spanning multiple engineering organizations for Infrastructure (Developer Infrastructure, Core Infrastructure, Service Infrastructure).

green_flags: Clearly defined scope across three infrastructure pillars (Developer, Core, Service Infrastructure)
Explicit encouragement to apply if minimum requirements are met
Clear separation of minimum vs. preferred qualifications
High-impact role embedded directly in Stripe's core payment systems
red_flags: No location, compensation, or work authorization information provided in the JD
No visa sponsorship information stated — candidates needing sponsorship cannot assess fit
Broad experience range (software engineering, systems engineering, OR TPM) makes it hard to calibrate exact seniority level expected

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from 5+ years requirement and scope of responsibilities; not explicitly stated)
scope.business_model (inferred from company description as financial infrastructure platform)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not directly stated in JD)
location.type (not stated in JD body; set to 'unclear')
ambiguities: The JD lists 'software engineering, systems engineering, or technical program management experience' as qualifying — it is unclear whether prior TPM experience is required or whether a pure engineering background is equally acceptable.
No location, office policy, or remote eligibility is mentioned anywhere in the JD. Work arrangement is fully unclear.
